title: veronica rockii subsp. rockii
intro: Veronica rockii subsp. rockii, also known as the Tibetan speedwell, is a plant species in the family Plantaginaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Tibetan medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce stress. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Veronica rockii subsp. rockii can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
